reviews.review_text: Civilization VI is one of those games where you tell yourself you'll only play for an hour, and suddenly it’s 3 AM. Every civilization feels unique, fun to play, and no two playthroughs ever feel the same. The replayability is honestly insane, especially with the DLC. Definitely one of the most addicting strategy games I’ve played.

Personal Rating: Very Positive (90%)

I spent my entire morning and afternoon by playing this today.

I used to really hate the hexagonal tile design, as it looked really dumb to me. But after giving it one more shot today for... whatever reason, I actually really like it.

By gameplay, it's somewhere in between the old AoH2 and HoI4, and that's pretty much ideal. Pretty challenging, but not overcomplicated.

I can see myself playing further, as it's really enjoyable and time passes on really quickly when playing. But I don't know if I'll go for Civ 7 or any of the DLC's.

After finally passing 1,000 hours playtime for this, I figured I should write a review.

Civilization games function best when they allow sandbox gameplay to tell a story on its own. From the very beginnings of each game, you feel invested in your civilization and can understand how beliefs / religion can form from the natural habitat around you and the benefits it offers. Starting near floodplains can lead to you worshipping the rivers and marshes and the food they bring, starting in harsher climates such as the desert or tundra can strongly alter the culture of a country. You can develop long lasting rivalries with opposing civilizations and bare grudges for hundreds of years over past slights.

Gameplay wise each of the different win conditions is varied enough to allow you to choose a different path through each playthrough. Domination can be a slow grind towards the end as you finish off the last opponents, religion seems like a slightly more limited version of domination but does have some variety. Science victory is the least reliant on interaction with the AI and so feels like a good fallback option if other objectives haven't gone your way early. Culture wins are the most varied simply because of all the different ways of getting culture / tourism and the different mechanics that feed into it.

The last Civilization game I played a lot of was 2, so I initially approached the combat in 6 with the same brute force unit spam tactics. Fortunately things have moved on since then, you have to use the terrain and the rock/paper/scissors mechanics of the unit types to your advantage. Only forts seem to be largely useless.

One of the best things about this game is how history is brought to life within it. It was a joy to pick up on all the familiar names, from Mary Shelley's / Jane Austen's / Leo Tolstoy's books to the paintings of Andrei Rublev (as I was aware through the Andrei Tarkovsky film of his life). Even figures I became aware of such as Kristina of Sweden (depicted by Liv Ulman in The Abdication) and Eleanor of Aquitane (depicted by Katharine Hepburn in The Lion In Winter). The more you learn about the different cultures, the more you feel tied to them in the game.

If I had to voice one frustration with the game, it's that the AI doesn't play the game well enough so the difficulty levels are compensated by giving them extreme bonuses in terms of unit numbers at the start of the game. This creates a slightly skewed gameplay curve where the biggest challenge is surviving and playing catchup at the start and yet by about halfway through your victory can feel somewhat inevitable because the AI is incapable of building districts correctly and focusing on win conditions.

Overall I can't recommend the game and all the expansions enough. I played for a while without the addons and felt satisfied, but once I got them it was like I had an almost entirely new game to learn and enjoy.

Played the game for a decent amount of time with no problem whatsoever for years. But now I can't just enjoy the game: LAN play is just failing on a random error and internet play is forbidden, probably because some braindead biggot dudes somewhere on the globe decided this game would endanger the brain of the poor teenagers. Thus internet play is gated behind an age verification that is poorly implemented as it says I do not meet the requirements, probably because I am not a fossilized Trump administration follower. 
To add insult to injury, I can play if I use the account of my 10 years youger cousin with steam families, that is a hell of a security flaw in an otherwise completely unsollicited design.  
The icing of the cake : the game is working perfectly fine on Linux, but they never fixed the crossplay with Microslop's dumptser OS. so I have to use this inferior version with proton in order to play with other people.
This was for 2K

To the developer team: game is great, would gladly put a positive review if your publisher was not as toxic and decided to change course, but given its history and the fact that the game is not active anymore, I do not think they will ever patch it.

I own both Civ 6 and Civ 7, I honestly prefer Civ 6. It's a very addictive game that causes time to fly, it's extremely fun alone and even more fun with friends. While it has many DLCs, the base game goes to as cheap as 6 dollars on sale and you can get the Anthology bundle (everything) for only like 35 on a good sale. All in all, highly recommend, extremely unique and fun game, will have you playing for hours.
this game is like crack cocaine, you want to play 2 more turns then its 3 hours later. If you can find a dedicated group of friends to play with (3+) its quite fun.

Das Spiel ist nett gemacht, aber viele Konzepte sind nicht durchdacht (Religion, Diplomate, usw.). Was aber gar nicht geht, sind minutenlanges Warten zwischen den Runden. Eigentlich eine Frechheit, das Spiel so zu verkaufen.

No clue why but the game now no longer works on my system. After resetting my entire computer the program still does not work. Thank you for not supporting your customers. Love the pre written responses to my request for assistance, I have decided I will never buy another game from you.

Don't get me wrong, this game is fun because it's Civ. The gameplay of Civ is inherently fun to me. But while the game has that going for it, everything else makes it worse. 

The more cartoonish artstyle makes it feel less serious to me. The UI and apperance of menus are better and more immersive in Civ V in my opinion. 

I generally feel that there's a feeling of higher quality in Civ V compared to this game.
Too colourful, too ugly, and too stale. While the district mechanic is interesting the game is inferior in every other aspect to Civ V with Lekmod, which also gets patched more frequently.

I enjoy this game a ton, including steam workshop. But in July 2025 their EULA/TOS changes grant 2K (and associates) access to parts of my PC that are not okay, such as social media history, web browser history, payment and passwords, and more. These go beyond privacy invasion and the excuse of wanting market data doesn't fly.

So I will be giving this game a thumbs down until things change.

There is no way one review could describe the scope of this game, but I will try anyways. Widely recognized as one of the greatest 4X games of all time, Civ 6 has it all. From the ancient past to the near future, you will spread religion, wage war, and compete in culture and science to be the greatest Civilization. I don't play a lot of video games (under an hour each day), but I have still played 200 hours of this. There is simply no other game that could possibly rival it. It is engaging throughout, easy to learn, and has plenty of challenges to keep you interested beyond your 100th hour.

As with any game, there are a few cons. The AI aren't the most challenging, and after you reach a certain skill level you will find that they are easy to beat at any difficulty. The game, for being 10 years old, is still at full price. While that's certainly justified, it could be a deterrent, except for the fact that it frequently goes on sale for under $10!

WARNING: THIS PRODUCT CONTAINS CIV6, A HIGHLY ADDICTIVE SUBSTANCE.
Perfect game to play if you have 5 hours to waste. The computer always hates me, no matter what I do. Perfection.

It has some things other civs don't. Would not consider it better than Civ 4 BTS, but its certainly better than the new Civ 7... Overall this is a great civ game and much prefer it over the new Civ 7 any day. Though nothing beats Civ 4 BTS. But again, this Civ (6) is truly good and wish they would of expanded on Civ 6 instead of creating that money grabber two-bid slosh scheme know as Civ 7
Ai sucks and is predictable after 500 hours.  Otherwise the game is legit perfection.  Get the DLCs for sure.

I have played this game since its beginning.  I remember playing the original off a 1.44 floppy disk from computer to computer. Civ has definately stood the test of time. I would get Civ 7, but the price is too high atm.
it is a very fun and creative game. strongly recommend.

I haven't played this game long enough to leave review on the game itself but a quick glance at the reviews showed me how much users are furious about the level of personal information this game takes away from you and it became obvious for me to leave a negative review. The level of personal information is something I would give only to the police or a therapist. This is simply unacceptable. 

If we do not hold off such greed without wallets or votes, things will only get worse.
very good game, had hours of fun but very complex for first time

Great game. I know I'll sink many more hours into it, but I'll admit, it would be best experienced with all the dlcs. Maybe wait for a sale to get it, but it is a genuinely fun game and may even inspire you to learn more about historical figures you do and don't recognize.

Civ 6 takes a long time to warm up to, and even longer to master. But ultimately I have found it to be one of the best entries in the Civ series so far. The cultures all seem unique, the combat is interesting, and the way the various victories and ages works are all very fun (and customizable!). I had a great deal of fun with the built-in Zombies mode, and the apocalypse modes as well. Go Rome or go home!

I've been playing Civilization since I was eight years old, Civ III was my first game, and I went back to play the others. This is by far the most player friendly, and mods make it so that if you enjoy the older Civ games more, you can have similar mechanics and graphics in this game. It's my favorite right now not just because of the graphics, but I really enjoy the newer mechanics as well. I still go back to play Civ V or Colonization from time to time, but Civ VI is a very solid game and well worth playing.
currently playing this game everything is over priced and the game made me lose 5 friends
Poor imbalanced strategy that gives the AI unfair advantages like faster building and more gold doesn't make a harder game it makes a broken unplayable one
Nearly 670 hours sunk into this game - there are still things that I am learning. I have so much more content to complete. A nice chill game to play at your own pace.

I love games that's a one purchase deal and everyone always start on equal footing. It's all on experience and skill that makes the difference. This is this game I'm still a noob after 400hr of game play lol.
Honestly this is my favourite Civ game. The art style, the mechanics, how complex and historically accurate everything is, you really can't improve on anything because it's perfect.

Civ 6 is just as fun as the other entries but I think they made this one a bit too easy. Pillaging for example is way too overpowered, you can use it to farm science and gold to the point that managing your finances becomes trivial. On top of that, the AI tends to have weak armies so after a certain point it's pretty easy to steam roll them. I still like most of the changes but I feel like the AI has trouble keeping up with them.

just a great game in every way even if it can be a little unbalanced its still the last civ that was actually authentic to real civilisations instead of civ 7 which is like build a bear for civilisations
spawning next to five barbarians as the ai subtly parks three warriors right next to my cities is good game design

Complex without much in the way of reward. Yes, you can keep going for hours but when you stop it doesn't feel like you've achieved anything. Every mechanic you could possibly think of is in the game, but they don't really make a coherent whole. I have never felt loth to stop playing, nor have I ever been excited to get back to the game.

For the first year after I bought the game I was unable to play because of a call stack error where the spaghetti code would dive into a stack of subroutines and then overwrite the first one and get completely lost when I tried to join any multiplayer game. My PC was perfectly able to run it in singleplayer and all the specs were up to standard, but only on a different PC did the problem go away. Test the game quickly if you do end up buying it to see if you need to refund the hefty price tag or if it will work for you.
One of the GOATs. Played it all the time and still enjoy it, just don't have the patience or time anymore. Maybe, just one more turn?
The best 'civiliztion building game you can buy! you will loose hours playing tho so be carefull

(3.8/5)

Civilization 6 is a wonderful grand strategy game that makes you feel like you're in control of an empire, planning trade routes, exchanging various goods with the other players, and constructing wonders for the world to see.

While it's not the most overwhelming Grand Strategy game I've played or is available, it can be a bit daunting at first as you must weed through district adjacencies, unit combat support, and other mechanics. Finding an easy beginner civ can also be challenging, especially with all of the DLC.

While the game is perfectly serviceable without any of the DLC, Rise & Fall and Gathering Storm change the game in very impactful ways, meaning that if you spend a lot of time without these and decide to pick them up at a later date (preferably on sale), a lot of your experience will not translate over.

As this game has a lot of DLC, some of which is pretty much mandatory for the full experience, I sadly cannot give it a 4*+, however it is well worth your time when on sale.

As a single player game there is enough going on that each new combination and random map makes it interesting. I hate the French all they do is spy and steal money, but otherwise the character of each leader plays a real role in development and style of play. I recommend but caution that you should always adjust the win conditions. More than once I have had higher difficulty wins by scores that leap ahead in the last few turns.
A lot of fun, some frustration, and the difficulty levels genuinely force alterations to play that make it a real challenge. Get the character packs because it makes it even more fun. No turn limit huge maps are awesome!
very good game, tons of replayability great with local multiplayer with friends while talking or just hanging out for a while
A lot of variety with what nations you can play, very fun with friends.
Have played both this and the newer version of Civ, and this one is so much better. I highly recommend this game to anyone who wants a game they can play for hours on end.
Can't finish a full game with my friends because we keep getting de-sync errors. Do not buy if you want to play with friends. CIV V works much better
A fantastic game and it has grown to be one of the best in the series. Defs worth every penny if you love strategy games
Easy to understand (started from tutorial) this games starts to evolve and expand with excellent pace and challenge. And you are not rushed which is great.
Upon witnessing me playing this game for a few hours, my best friend determined I should never be allowed to be in a position of power ever. 10/10
Prepare to lose the rest of your life to this game. Turn back! Don't buy, unless you want to be entertained for  thousands of hours

I've played A LOT. My word of Advice? Play like a Noob. No Mod's, No changing the settings etc... When you start manipulating the game you can basically make yourself Win and it ruins all the fun. I play on Deity and it is HARD. HARD AF. I think I've won 2 out my last 100 plays.
Those wins though? Sooo worth it. 
The game is fantastic. Taught me a lot about "Dialing back" my knowledge and think differently. Gameplay I give it a 9/10
Игра говна, балнс делал додик из Мумбаи, уебки играющие в эту игру больше 8 часов инцелы без перспектив в жизни

I've played almost every Civ since Civ 1.  The learning curve on Civ 6 is a little steep but it's really fun once you got it going.  Like most Civs, the AI is not great at complex warfare and other items, but still fun regardless!

Great game, a lot of fun to play.  Lots to learn, always something new to learn, but good online sources of information.  Sometimes buggy with victory conditions in multi player.  I'm in a game currently with all conditions met for a Culture victory, and it does not give me that victory.  I had a similar issue with a Religious victory once too.

I've been playing Civilization since the 1990s, and this sixth installment is my favorite yet. It builds on the strengths of previous iterations while also introducing new mechanisms to keep the franchise exciting and fresh, and allow for more creative and flexible strategies.
I mostly play this game on my switch but the extra stuff you get from PC is sick an absolutely great game and it really makes you use your brain
Favorite and most played game in my library.  My chill single player game that I keep coming back to.  Still holding off in VII
What a wonderful game, truly a masterpiece. I spent countless hours playing it, resulting in so many sleepless nights!

Never played a 4X game before, and it's a lot of fun. Took a minute to learn, lost seemingly randomly to culture and science victories at first, but once I got the hang of it it became a lot of fun and super addictive.
really great game! I am kind of addicted to this game, but the map editor seems to have many bugs, can you guys fix that?

Very interesting and relazing turn based strategy game. There are many different civilizations and scenarios to play with. But honestly after a while all matches start to resemble one another. Still recommended.

Got this game for $3 about 2 years ago, got the anthology edition for like $13 after playing a bit and falling in love with the game. Made it a goal to 100% the achievements: as of now (77% of achievements with 905 hours). dear god. help me.

Also Hungary's theme goes crazy
Great slow pace game with a ton of of different civilizations to choose from. Many different strategies to play!
This game is a once in a decade game. The story, the graphics, the artistry, the music, the  gameplay, everything about this is a work of art in a world full of slop games.
Best Civilization game out there. Yes some people do not like some of the features to this game but mainly it is because it is challenging and you really have to strategize in this game. 10/10
really interesting game, which leads from 15 min game to "one more turn" after 8 hours of playing :DDD
idk the game is cool i guess. not too much replayability but you can get a couple good hours out of it, worth a purchase
the game was amazing it was extensive with no major bugs highly recomend

Since I was a kid, I’ve always wanted to get into RPGs, simulations, turn-based, and turn-based strategy/tactics games, as I’ve always been drawn to them, but I just found them very complicated. A lot of things just went over my head. I would play games like Sims, Risk, and Bloons TD to scratch the itch, and while very fun, I still wanted more.

In 2020, I would heavily get into RPG turn-based combat games like FFN, Risk, XCOM, Slay the Spire, Persona, and Baldur's Gate. I hated how long it took me to give them a chance, and I don't want to repeat the same thing with turn-based strategy and RTS games. The games give off the same vibes as the RPGs, but just feel overwhelming as hell. I’ve bought several, but every time I boot them up, I feel like I’m reading a history textbook mixed with a spreadsheet simulator. There are just so many systems, mechanics, menus, and things to keep track of. They feel like shows that are on season 20, like, who the hell is Stussy? The barrier to entry is just too high. I feel like I need to familiarize myself with the older games. Despite all that, at the same time, I’m still weirdly drawn to them. RTS aside, there's just something special about building up a civilization, managing cities, going to war, making alliances, and watching your empire evolve. I just know that these are one of those games where, once the mechanics finally click for me, I’m probably going to get addicted and dump thousands of hours into them.

I could be wrong, but Civ seems like the type of game you can chill and play solo while listening to music or talking with friends. I’m also curious to know if it’s fun with friends too, kind of like games like Monopoly Plus and Risk, where everyone can just hop in, mess around, betray each other, and have a good time together. I’m just overwhelmed at the moment, and I don't really know what to do. Like, should I go back and play the earlier games, or is there a video that explains everything...?

Systems and Depth: 9.5/10 (Extremely rich in regional planning, government systems, and governor mechanics; easy to start but hard to master)  
War and Conquest: 8.5/10 (Tactical gameplay in the early and mid-game is excellent, but late-game high-level strikes are overly cumbersome)  
AI and Diplomacy: 6.0/10 (AI military and diplomatic logic is still a weak point; high difficulty relies entirely on cheat bonuses)  
Late-Game Experience: 7.0/10 (The 'trash time' is too long, workload is heavy, lacks endgame challenges)  
Addictiveness: 10/10 ('Just one more turn' magic is unbeatable, a true time killer)  

In 400 hours, I have led Roman legions to conquer the Eurasian continent, and I have also used Age of Exploration fleets to break open the doors to the Americas; I have calculated every patch of frozen land in the cold wind, and hesitated over pressing the nuclear button on whether to sink Venice.  
Civilization VI is not a perfect game; its AI sometimes acts like an idiot, and its late game sometimes feels like a factory line for clocking in. Yet it remains the grandest, most detailed 4X strategy game currently on the market, best fulfilling players' fantasy of 'creating history.' It condenses thousands of years of human civilization into a hexagonal board, allowing you to experience the epic feel from making fire to exploring space.  

If you have not tried the Civilization series yet, or just want to buy a game that can provide hundreds of hours of play, buying it is absolutely worth it. But please be sure: before clicking 'Start Game,' set your alarm, prepare liver supplements, and forgive yourself for not getting out of bed tomorrow morning.  

I think I can play just one more game. Just one, I’ll go to sleep after finishing this turn…
its alittle complicated when it comes to winning but apparently there are alot of strategies in the game to win, i don't know them all but its interesting to say in the least.
When playing online with friends or multiplayer it almost feels like playing a board game.
The game takes hours to finish, just to get beat by the computer, no matter the difficulty, you will always lose. Not like the other sid meyers games, don't waste your time.
Amazing turn based game for the best single player experience.

I have zero experience with this franchise or even genre of game, but playing through the tutorial had me hooked. It's pretty easy to grasp and not super intimidating, other than how much time it takes to play through.

so i dont like this game. wrong genre for me but i had to try it. yolo. 
having said that i admit it is a good well thought out well crafter game

One of my favorite games to pick up and put down endlessly. The game mechanics are so natural that I could never forget them and never really needed to learn most of them. The experience of going through a full round is incredibly fulfilling, I can never get enough. I have some weeks played through well over a dozen games of this.

My only complaint (which is now rendered less relevant given the frequency of updates) is the large dependence on paid dlc. I first played on my Nintendo Switch for some 80 hours, but my enjoyment only reached peak levels when buying the heavily discounted Anthology version here on Steam. Without the DLC, the game feels incomplete, which may serve as a compliment to the DLC or an insult to the base game. The jury's still out.

Very good game. Still needs more options . I wish there was a marathon speed with more time to research but less time to build units and buildings ,so to have the time to play with each era's different units war instead of fast research and the game still ends quick.

Fun and easy to sink hours into. Started at 10 PM, looked outside and it was morning. 

10/10, I will never play this game again.
Lots of variety in this version of Civilization. Leader choices and game mechanics keep me interested and engaged most of the time.

More cartoony than Civ V, but has a few extra mechanics such as loyalty, natural disasters and the District system. I would still prefer to play this until they get their shiznat figured out with Civ 7, as I've heard others have regretted that purchase.

This is my second attempt at this game. It's annoying there is no way to have the natural disasters without the pointless global forum where you can vote on things that affect civs you've never even met yet, and vice versa. It's very easy to fall into a Dark Age, the mechanics are quite opaque, and the whole thing just feels painfully slow even on the fastest mode compared to Civ V. Just going to uninstall it and get my drive space back again I guess.

It's Civ - and sure it had a rocky start when it was released but the franchise is a classic. But to be clear, if you time traveled to see how this game was at release -  No one liked the new mechanics. Poor thing was review bombed by purists still clinging on to Civ V. It's funny how at the end of it's lifespan NOW it's appreciated. And you know what? looks to be the EXACT same story with VII.

GAMERS RUIN GAMES.
I love this game. It is a lot of fun and I am old and that's OK. Love Jonathan BLTs.
This game is not a single story that you play once and then get bored with.  It's different every time you play it, and it's very engaging.
Really good and fun if you like building armies and taking over everything.
Very cool and complex game, i have 8h playtime and i have like 20% of this game discovered
Possibly a little easier than Civ V, once you get the hang of the new mechanics, possibly a little more fun/ replayable.

It's been months, years even that I've been playing this game. Never gets boring at all. Infact, each new game is a whole another level of experience.
Also, Zombies are insanely difficult.

Like any Civ game its easy to get lost in the campaign (absorbed into the process). Mechanically its probably not quite as intricate as the previous games but it is still fun imo and does have some interesting aspects.

Best game ever made! Actually reinstalling it after failing to adapt to the new CIV 7. Yah the graphics are better but I still enjoy the old mechanics better. Like putting on an old pair of boots. It just feels more comfortable.
Great game. Earth Huge with Barbarians turned off is great for single player!

After playing Civ 7 even with the update that allows you to play as one civ through all ages, I had to come back and give Civ 6 a positive review. Civilization has always been about standing the test of time. Civ 7 takes away everything that makes Civ 6 such a good game. Resources mean something in Civ 6. They have value and weight by unlocking tiers of military and economy might. You might even go to war just to obtain those special strategic resources to make sure you were able to keep up. Civ 6 has 7 beat in almost every measure....
Excellent strategy game that is also fun and rewarding.
After having won a game on deity with all leaders now i can say this game is good and fun

Devs cooked and forgot the recipe between Civ VI and Civ VII.

I found Civ VI a little overwhelming at first as someone new to turn-based large scale strategy games, (though found it an excellent warmup for Stellaris), but after a few short playthroughs, I got it down.

I know a lot of people don't like the clay-like graphics, but personally, I think it makes everything softer and easier on the eyes when playing for 5+ hour sessions. The game has lovely pacing and even some storytelling. UI isn't too cluttered and you have the option to micromanage cities if you want/need.

My only real criticism is the odd currency inflation which happens with units when you hoard currency a little too much. You almost have to constantly be expending currency to keep units at a stable price for on-demand purchase.

Lots of DLC's, lots of differing perks per Civ, lots of game customization and control, and lots of fun. I highly recommend this game if you enjoy strategy games, and I recommend this above all other Civ games. Also, like I said earlier, this is a pretty good prelude to Stellaris in practicing grand strategy and attention.

Also, multiplayer sucks. I tried it and it's unfortunately hella buggy. Plus the game takes twice as long between two, as less stuff is automated between you and other players. Multiplayer is defo a gripe of mine.
Addicting, can't stop telling myself one more turn only to play like 20 more
great game. i bought the dlcs and it made it so much better. love hojo tokimune and overall amazing game. 10/10.
Dette herre e det beste spillet i har spelt nogon sinne, fyyyyyy faden i elske det.
There are complaints about Civ 6 with M4 apple chip going back several years. They want me to buy a new game? Support your last one on what you say you support it on.

The amount of DLC's is stupid but the modding community is amazing. Can't think of any other game where I can play against the Fire keeper from Dark Souls at the same time as a genshin impact character is my ally and Donald Trump is a warmonger. Wish there was a Hitler mod.
it was good but DO NOT DO WORLD BUILDER it will crash and break the game
Civilization is the greatest game franchise of all time, and this iteration is perfectly worthy of that legacy

Civ VI is one of the best games strategy games I have played. Playing with friends is even more fun! I love blowing up my opponents stupidly placed island nation while he is helpless to do anything about it. Would reccomend if you don't necessarily enjoy your current friendships.
Civ 5 was fantastic for a beginner. Civ 6 for a beginner is a living hell, even if you play on the Settler level.

Turned based grand strategy games... well thats my bag.  But Im not discerning when it comes to game features pros and cons.  I only know what I keep coming back to and what I dont.  Civ 1, WoW,  EU4, Starcraft, Tacticus have done it for me over the many years.  This game also has balanced its many features and numbers and stats with the much more important ability to slowly improve in the game by feeling your way through it.   Depth is there, but you can also just wing it if thats your thing. Love it.
